response:
to find the denominator we must store the count of prime factors.
***
the number ten contains prime factors two and five to store.
***
we need to store how many fives are in the factorial.
***
counting the fives gives us twenty four plus four which is twenty eight.
***
the power of ten requires fifty factors of five to store.
***
since we have only twenty eight fives we cannot store enough.
***
the remaining fives needed come from the denominator of the fraction.
***
we must store the difference between fifty and twenty eight.
***
this difference is twenty two so the denominator will be five to the power twenty two.
***
finally we store the result as five raised to the twenty second power.
